previous_statement: UNIVERSAL/DIMENSION-FREE CONSTANTS AND NO COMPLETE POSITIVITY. (a) Universal & dimension-free: each cited constant is universal and dimension-free -- C_1,C_2,C_3 from GT-easy (node 1.2-1.5), C_4 from GT-jordan (node 1.6), and the order-unit identity of GT-orderunit (node 1.1) is exact with no constant; the eta_0<1/4 of GT-Pprops is universal. Node 1.7 sets C=max(C_1,C_2,C_3,C_4) and eta_0=1/4, both universal and independent of dim H. (b) No complete positivity: every input uses ONLY positivity+unitality of Phi and the operator-norm hypothesis ||Phi^2-Phi||<=eta. Explicitly: GT-Pprops (node 1.1 setup) builds P=theta(2Phi-1) by the binomial series with NO CP; GT-easy (nodes 1.2-1.5) uses Jordan-Schwarz for the unital positive map Phi (a positivity, not CP, inequality) and delta-positivity of P; GT-jordan (node 1.6) is the one-hole/square-hole estimate, again CP-free. The amplification id_n (x) Phi is never invoked. Hence the whole assembly is CP-FREE, as required by the contract.
new_statement: Constants universal & dimension-free: with eta_0 < 1/4 FIXED (strict), the GT-Pprops constant C(eta_0)=sum_{n>=1}|a_n|(4 eta_0)^{n-1} (a_n the binomial coefficients of x^{-1/2}) is FINITE because 4 eta_0<1 (inside the radius of convergence rho=1); hence every C_i built from it is finite, universal, and dimension-free (no dependence on dim H or rank). At the EXCLUDED boundary eta_0=1/4 (4 eta_0=1) the series diverges — which is precisely why eta_0<1/4 must be strict. AND no complete positivity is used anywhere: the binomial P, Jordan-Schwarz, and the one-hole estimates each use only positivity+unitality (CP-free per their validated workspaces); the assembly never invokes an amplification id_n (x) Phi.
